samples.xml_description:
Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers. Clock signal. Reset signal. Signed or unsigned operation. Input operand DIVIDEND. Input operand DIVISOR. Valid operation request. The output has already been consumed. The result is valid. Output result. 0 1 2 3 4 posedge clk div_valid = 1'b0; div_valid = res_valid & res_ready ? 1'b0 : 1'b1;

Implement a 16-bit divider that accepts two 8-bit operands.

Inputs are two 8-bit operands.

Outputs are a 16-bit result.

Output valid when the operation is complete.

Design a finite-state machine to control the operation.

Design the data path to perform the operation.

Support both signed and unsigned operations.

Use a shift register.

Use a counter to control the data path.

Use a multiplexer to select the result.

Use a register to store the result.

Use a register to store the counter.

Use a register to store the shift register.

Use a register to store the divisor.

Use a register to store the dividend.

Use a register to store the negated divisor.

Use a register to store the remainder.

Use a register to store the quotient.

Use a register to store the sign.

Use a register to store the subtraction result.

Use a register to store the carry-out.

Use a register to store the carry-in.

Use a register to store the shift amount.

Use a register to store the shift register.

Use a register to store the shift register input.

Use a register to store the shift register output.

Use a register to store the temporary result.

Use a register to store the final result.

Reset all registers on reset.

Start the operation when the operation is valid.

Perform subtraction to calculate the remainder.

Use a multiplexer to select the input for the shift register.

Shift the remainder left by one bit.

Shift the quotient right by one bit.

Use a register to store the number of bits to shift.

Use a register to store the shift amount.

Use a register to store the shift register.

Use a register to store the shift register input.

Use a register to store the shift register output.

Use a register to store the temporary result.

Use a register to store the final result.

Use a register to store the number of bits to shift.

Use a register to store the shift amount.

Use a register to store the shift register.

Use a register to store the shift register input.

Use a register to store the shift register output.

Use a register to store the temporary result.

Use a register to store the final result.

Shift the remainder left by one bit.

Shift the quotient right by one bit.

Repeat until the remainder is less than the divisor.

Output the remainder and the quotient.

The quotient is the number of times the divisor can be subtracted from the remainder.

The remainder is the remaining value after the divisor has been subtracted from the dividend.

Output the remainder and the quotient as the result.

The output is valid when the operation is complete.
